topic.what: Merge pre-trained private models to satisfy flexible target privacy requirements without retraining
topic.how: Random selection (RS) sampling from model distribution and linear combination (LC) of model weights with RDP/PLD privacy accounting
topic.why_matters: Enables modular privacy adaptation at deployment time without data access or additional DP-SGD overhead

characteristics.context: δ=10⁻⁵, comparing RS/LC under RDP/PLD accounting

characteristics.what_it_measures: ε privacy budget under PLD accounting
characteristics.context: PLD consistently yields smaller ε than RDP for same accuracy

characteristics.what_it_measures: Mean squared error in Gaussian mean estimation
characteristics.context: LC achieves MSE ≤ RS under same privacy budget

requires: known training algorithms (for tight LC bounds)

novelty: First systematic study of private model merging under DP constraints
Joint RDP/PLD accounting for random selection (Theorem 5.1, 5.2)
Tight LC privacy accounting under DP-SGD structure (Theorem 6.2)
No-free-lunch result showing mechanism-agnostic merging limitations (Proposition 3.1)
open_problems: Designing sophisticated merging rules for nonconvex models
Private procedures for tuning merging parameters (λ, π)
Extensions to handle correlated checkpoint inputs
Tighter LC bounds under minimal assumptions
